lines.en_lit: there was the seat of the image of jealousy, which provokes jealousy” “And he brought me into the inner court of the Lord’s House, and behold at the opening of the Entrance Hall of God, between the porch and the altar were about twenty-five men with their backs toward the Temple of the Lord, and their faces toward the east, and they worshipped the sun toward the east” From the fact that it is stated “and their faces toward the east,” is it not clear that their backs were to the Sanctuary, Rather, what the verse states “their backs toward the Temple of the Lord”? This teaches that they would uncover and defecate downward,

The Holy One, Blessed be He, said to Michael, Michael, your nation has sinned He replied: Master of the Universe, it be enough for the good among them He said to him: I will burn them and the good among them Immediately, “He spoke to the man clothed in linen and said: Go in between the wheelwork and beneath the cherub, and fill your hands with coals of fire from between the cherubs, and scatter them over the city; and he came before my eyes” Immediately: “And the cherub stretched out his hand from between the cherubs into the fire that was between the cherubs, and took and put it into the hands of him that was clothed in linen, who took it and went out”

Rav Ḥana bar Bizna said Rabbi Shimon Ḥasida said: If it were not for the fact that the embers cooled from the hand of the cherub to the hand of Gabriel, not a remnant or a refugee of the enemies of the Jewish people, would have survived.

And it is written: “And behold, the man clothed in linen with the slate by his side, reported the matter saying: I have done as You have commanded me” Rabbi Yoḥanan said: At that moment, they cast out Gabriel from behind the curtain [pargod], and they struck him with sixty blows [pulsei] of fire. They said to him: If you did not do it, you did not do it; if you did do it, why did you not do it according to what you were commanded Moreover, you already did it, do you not have One should not deliver about destruction?

They brought Dubiel, the ministering angel of the Persians and put him in the place of [baḥarikei] and he served for twenty-one days. As it is written: “But the prince of the kingdom of Persia stood opposed to me for twenty-one days, but, lo, Michael, one of the chief princes, came to help me and I remained there beside the kings of Persia” they gave him, twenty-one kings the seaport Mashhig.

said: Write for me that the Jews taxes [akarga] They wrote it for him Write for me that the Sages taxes. They wrote for him. When they wanted to sign Gabriel stood from behind the curtain and said: “It is vain for you who rise early who sit up late to eat the bread of sorrow, for He gives His beloved sleep” What “for He gives His beloved sleep” Rav Yitzḥak said: These are the wives of Torah scholars who disturb sleep in this world and merit the World-to-Come. They did not listen to

He said before Him: Master of the Universe, if all the wise men of other nations were placed on one side of the scale, and Daniel the beloved man were on the other side, would he not outweigh them? The Holy One, Blessed be He, said: Who is the one who teaches the virtue of My children? They said to Him: Master of the Universe, Gabriel. He said to them: Let him come is stated: “And I have come due to your words” God said Let him ascend. They brought him up.

He came found Dubiel holding the letter in his hand. wanted to take from him, swallowed it. Some say the letter was written, but it was not signed. Some say it was also signed, when he swallowed it, was erased. This is in the kingdom of Persia, there are who pay taxes and there are who do not pay taxes, “And when I depart from him, the prince of Greece comes” screamed and screamed but no one listened to him.

If you wish, say that bathing is considered affliction, from where do we As it is written: “And to Ebiathar the priest the king said: Get to Anatoth to your fields, for you are deserving of death. But I will not put you to death today, because you carried the Ark of the Lord God before David my father, and because you have been afflicted in all that my father was afflicted” And it is written with regard to David: “For they said the people is hungry, and weary, and thirsty in the wilderness” Hunger from bread and thirst from water weary from what? Is it not from bathing? And perhaps wearing shoes?
Rather, Rav Yitzḥak said: from here: “As cold water on a weary soul, But perhaps from drinking? Is it written: As cold water in a weary soul? “on a weary soul” is written,

wearing shoes from where do we As it is written: “And David went up by the ascent of the Mount of Olives, and wept as he went up, and he had his head covered, and was walking barefoot” Barefoot of what? Is it not of wearing shoes? perhaps from a horse and whip.

Rather, Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: from here, “Go and loose the sackcloth from your loins, and remove your shoe from your foot” And it is written: “And he did so, walking naked and barefoot” Barefoot of what? Is it not wearing shoes? And say with patched shoes. Because if you do not say this, actually naked? Rather, ragged garments. Here too, in patched shoes.

Rather, Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said from here: “Withhold your foot from being barefoot, and your throat from thirst” Keep yourself from sin, so that your feet will not come to be barefoot; keep your tongue from idle talk, so that your throat will not come to be thirsty.
From where do we conjugal relations is called affliction? As it is written, “If you shall afflict my daughters, and if you shall take wives
